content.problemStatement:
Chef loves problems about digits and he came up with an interesting one.

Chef has given you two integers NNN and KKK. You should find the **minimum** non-negative integer XXX, such that N+XN + XN+X has **at most** KKK distinct digits in its decimal representation.

For example, 123112311231 has three distinct digits and 100100100 has two distinct digits.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case consists of two space-separated integers NNN and KKK, as described in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **minimum** non-negative integer XXX which satisfies the problem conditions.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: 303030 has 222 distinct digits which is more than 111, so we should add 333 to 303030 to make it equal to 333333 which has only 111 distinct digit.
